analysis_design.candidate_direction_basis: known dog ancestry in the captive backcross, independent of SNPs
analysis_design.excluded_desert_mallee_controls: The headerless analysis metadata labels desert=58 and mallee=74, while paper Table 2 reports Desert=74 and Mallee=58; the geolocation file has Desert=77, Mallee=55, and three NIL rows absent from the analysis metadata. The Alpine/backcross/dog positive panel is unaffected; Desert and Mallee controls are excluded.

excluded_known_F1_group.label: hybrid
excluded_known_F1_group.reason: 14 maximum diploid copies cannot support the frozen g=2..16 grid

direction_head.model: StandardScaler plus multinomial logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
direction_head.representation: mean and population SD over g=2..16 for each of 27 non-depth coordinates; identical to the external-panel depth grid
direction_head.score_interpretation: uncalibrated out-of-distribution score; not a probability or posterior
direction_head.training_selection: all positive canonical replicates at every migration rate

gate_head.model: StandardScaler plus binary logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
gate_head.representation: selected depth rows plus across-depth mean of 27 non-depth coordinates
gate_head.score_interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not the canonical 243-D gate probability
gate_head.training_target: positive migration with rate >=2.5e-4 versus weak positives plus control

guardrail: One pedigree-anchored, eight-animal dog-component stress-test cohort with proxy endpoints; the cross is not an exclusive population-level single-edge history. Four source/locus filters are correlated sensitivities. Raw severe-OOD scores are not accepted calls, probabilities, ancestry estimates, or migration rates.

outcome.accuracy_guardrail: The pedigree anchors the dog-introgressing component, not an exclusive one-edge population history or an accuracy rate; four filters reuse the same eight P2 animals and reference samples.

adjudication.guardrail: Pedigree establishes dog ancestry independently of SNPs, but this backcross is not an exclusive population-level single-edge history. The potentially related cohort is one biological unit, uses proxy references, and filter scopes are not independent trials.
adjudication.severe_OOD_rule: max(direction RMS-z, gate RMS-z) > 10; heuristic diagnostic, not calibrated support

external_expectation.candidate_direction_basis: documented dog ancestry in the captive backcross, independent of SNP genotypes
external_expectation.candidate_forward_direction: domestic dog P3 -> captive backcross P2
external_expectation.pedigree: (dingo x dog) F1 hybrid x dingo
external_expectation.reference_caveat: Alpine dingoes and pooled dogs are proxy endpoints, not documented individual parents
external_expectation.relatedness_caveat: the release does not establish eight independent families
external_expectation.tree_contract_status: operational proxy order only: P2 is an admixed backcross cohort and P1/P3 are proxy references rather than established tree leaves or documented parents

P2_complete_case_ascertainment.guardrail: Because P2 has exactly eight diploids, the frozen g=16 requirement removes every locus with any missing P2 genotype; the analyzed row set is therefore conditioned on complete P2 calls.

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called across every declared three-population panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

input_audit.source_filter_interpretation: all 2,466 rows reported as the paper's Australian SNP dataset; inherited FILTER tags normalized

model_free_comparator.interpretation: reference-invariant descriptive geometry; projection is not bounded ancestry, and neither projection nor f3 estimates temporal direction

scaffold_block_bootstrap.guardrail: descriptive linked-locus sensitivity only; case ascertainment and label reuse remain
scaffold_block_bootstrap.method: nonparametric scaffold-block bootstrap with equal scaffold resampling

simulation_feature_shift.reference: StandardScaler fitted to 2,700 positive regen_full summaries on the identical g=2..16 grid

simulation_gate.interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not a probability or posterior

simulation_head.interpretation: uncalibrated OOD scores; not probabilities or posteriors

runtime.process_priority: BelowNormal requested at runner start on Windows

geolocation_audit.release_discrepancy: The headerless analysis metadata labels desert=58 and mallee=74, while paper Table 2 reports Desert=74 and Mallee=58; the geolocation file has Desert=77, Mallee=55, and three NIL rows absent from the analysis metadata. The Alpine/backcross/dog positive panel is unaffected; Desert and Mallee controls are excluded.

release_filter_normalization.operation: set FILTER=PASS on every released data row; no other field changed
release_filter_normalization.rationale: The paper reports all 2,466 released SNPs as its Australian dataset. This scope reproduces that row set; the separate vcf_PASS_only scope retains the source FILTER tags.

analysis_design.P2: back: 8 captive (dingo x dog) F1 hybrid x dingo backcrosses
analysis_design.candidate_direction_basis: documented dog ancestry in the captive backcross, independent of SNP genotypes
analysis_design.candidate_forward_direction: domestic dog P3 to captive backcross P2
guardrails: The eight backcrosses are one small, potentially related cohort; filters are correlated sensitivities, not trials.
Alpine dingoes and pooled domestic dogs are proxy endpoints, not documented individual parents.
The pedigree establishes a dog-introgressing component, not an exclusive population-level single-edge history or a formal accuracy datum.
The seven known F1 hybrids have only 14 maximum diploid copies and cannot support the frozen g=2..16 grid.
Do not use SNP-inferred free-ranging backcross labels from older dingo work as truth; Weeks et al. argue population structure and incomplete lineage sorting can mimic that signal.

paper.title: Genetic structure and common ancestry expose the dingo-dog hybrid myth

release_discrepancies: The analysis metadata labels desert=58 and mallee=74, while paper Table 2 reports Desert=74 and Mallee=58.
The geolocation file has Desert=77, Mallee=55, and three NIL IDs absent from the 434-sample analysis metadata.

The 2,466-row VCF retains 233 inherited non-PASS VQSR tranche tags although the paper reports all 2,466 SNPs as the Australian analysis dataset; the benchmark reports both the paper row set and a VCF-PASS-only sensitivity.
